name: Icon
summary: Renders an Iconify icon by name (`icon`) or a safe inline SVG (`svg`); arbitrary SVG strings go through an allowlist parser, so use the explicit `dangerouslySetSvg` escape hatch only for trusted full-SVG markup. Size and color follow the surrounding text.

props.name: svg
props.description:
Custom SVG:
• **string** – passed through the parseSvgString allowlist
  parser. Accepted forms: bare path `d`-data (`"M12 2L2 22h20z"`,
  wrapped in a 24×24 viewBox) or `<path>`-only markup, optionally
  inside one `<svg>` wrapper. The result is rendered as real React
  `<svg>`/`<path>` elements — never via `innerHTML`. Anything outside
  that grammar renders nothing and dev-warns.
• **ReactElement** – a full `<svg>` element you already trust.

**BREAKING (Q6):** full-SVG strings (`<svg>…</svg>` containing anything
other than `<path>`, or any string with entities/comments/scripts) no
longer render here — they are an XSS vector through the old
`dangerouslySetInnerHTML` sink. For trusted full-SVG markup, pass a
ReactElement or use IconProps.dangerouslySetSvg.

props.name: dangerouslySetSvg
props.description:
Escape hatch for **trusted** raw SVG markup. Reproduces the pre-0.35
`svg` string behavior exactly: the markup is injected verbatim via
`dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, so it can execute scripts/event handlers and
load remote references. The name is the warning — only ever pass markup
you fully control (never model output or user input). For untrusted or
agent-generated markup use IconProps.svg, which is parsed.

props.name: size
props.description: Icon size token or explicit CSS size.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: Prefer well‑known glyphs and keep sizes consistent with surrounding text or controls.
Provide accessible labels when icons are actionable or convey status.
Avoid using colour alone to convey meaning; pair with text or status tokens.
Use tokens/presets for colour and size; avoid ad‑hoc CSS to preserve consistency.
Don’t overload meaning—keep one concept per icon and avoid ambiguous metaphors.

For a custom `svg` string, pass bare path `d`-data or `<path>`-only markup: it is parsed against an allowlist and rendered as real React elements, never via innerHTML. Untrusted or model-generated icon markup is safe here—anything outside that grammar renders nothing and dev-warns.
Only use `dangerouslySetSvg` for markup you fully control. It injects raw SVG verbatim (innerHTML) and can execute scripts—never pass user input or AI output to it.
